cards.title: Fee matching and applicability
instructions: Use the public fees.json schema exactly. Empty list fields mean the fee rule applies to all values for that dimension.
Compute fee_eur as fixed_amount plus rate times eur_amount divided by 10000. The rate field is in basis points.
Resolve merchant profile fields before filtering transaction rows. Merchant-level account type, MCC, capture delay, and acquirer fields are not payment-level fields.
When a question asks for applicable fee IDs, return the matching fee IDs as integers in ascending order unless the guidelines specify another order.
When a question asks which merchants are affected by a fee rule or a fee-rule restriction, compare fee applicability over the same yearly transaction population.
verification_checks: Check merchant-level filters before payment-level filters.

cards.title: Fee simulation and counterfactual changes
instructions: For counterfactual questions, build one baseline calculation and one modified calculation with the same filters and aggregation semantics.
For rate-change deltas, fixed_amount cancels when only rate changes. The delta is the rate difference times eur_amount divided by 10000 over the affected transactions.
For relative-only fee simulations, set the affected fee rule fixed_amount to 0 and keep the rate field unchanged over the same matching transactions.
For traffic-steering questions, apply the hypothetical scheme or attribute to the evaluated transactions before matching fee rules.
When a question asks for total fees, sum every matching fee rule per transaction unless the task explicitly asks for a single best or average fee.

cards.title: Fraud, shopper, and customer metrics
instructions: Use the manual and payments-readme definitions before choosing numerator and denominator for fraud or shopper metrics.
For fraud-rate questions, verify whether the requested rate is by EUR volume, transaction count, or grouped aggregation.
For unique-email metrics, decide explicitly how missing email addresses should be handled from the task wording.
For repeat-customer metrics, define the customer key and the transaction window before computing counts or percentages.

cards.title: Schema domain and manual-defined values
instructions: For field-domain questions, combine values defined in the public manual with values observed in the data when the task asks for possible values.
Use merchant_category_codes.csv to resolve MCC descriptions to numeric MCC codes before fee filtering.
For date-window questions, convert calendar dates to the dataset date representation and use inclusive bounds when the task names an inclusive period.
For duplicate-row questions, use the exact column scope requested by the task before calling duplicated.

cards.title: DABStep answer formatting contracts
instructions: Return only the requested value in agent_answer. Do not include units, explanations, or extra labels unless the guideline requests them.
For comma-separated lists, use a comma followed by one space between items.
For grouped list outputs, follow the bracket, separator, sorting, and rounding instructions in the guideline exactly.
For numeric answers, round only after the calculation and format to the requested number of decimals.
